reasoning:
For the market to resolve YES, Tesla needs to have completed at least 10,000 bona fide driverless ride-hailing trips by the end of 2025. The market explicitly excludes rides that have a human backup, require intervention, or are just for testing (no Tesla-employed testers, no observers, and must be genuine trips >400m).

Tesla launched a supervised a ride-hailing app and service in June 2025 in Austin, Texas. However, multiple news reports confirm that until mid-December 2025, these vehicles operated with human safety monitors or drivers in the vehicle. On December 15, 2025, Elon Musk announced that Tesla had just started testing the robotaxis with no occupants/safety drivers in Austin. 

To meet the criteria, Tesla would have to go from the very first tests of empty driverless vehicles on December 15 to completing 10,000 genuine customer/non-tester rides by December 31, 2025. Doing this in 16 days would require completing over 600 bona fide driverless passenger rides per day starting immediately on day one of testing, which is logistically unfeasible for a brand-new driverless testing program that is just starting with a small fleet in a single city. Furthermore, the reports specifically phrase this latest step as "testing ... with no occupants in the car", meaning they likely haven't even started picking up actual passengers yet.

Because of this rigid timeline constraint and the strictness of the resolution criteria, it is virtually impossible for Tesla to reach 10,000 qualifying trips by the end of the year.
